html:
Exclusive to:  

The Bundling Wrap is a Survival Tab Item that can only be prototyped by using its Blueprint. It requires 1 Wax Paper and 1 Rope to craft, and is used to access 4 storage slots that can be wrapped up to create Bundled Supplies holding any items placed within.

Its Blueprint has a 4% chance to drop from a killed Bee or Killer Bee. Several items cannot be put inside Bundled Supplies, such as itself and Chester's Eye Bone. Since Honeycomb is not renewable in Don't Starve, only a limited number of Bundling Wrap can be crafted per world.

Bundled Supplies

Bundled Supplies are created by wrapping up to 4 stacks of items with a Bundling Wrap. It can be stored in Chests, Backpacks or the player's Inventory. Its appearance changes based on the number of storage slots used when wrapping items, regardless of the size of the stack in any slot or the type of item placed inside. Food items can be stored inside Bundled Supplies indefinitely without spoiling, providing a great deal of utility for survival. Unwrapping it will cause all the items stored inside to drop on ground, in addition to 1 Wax Paper.


×1-4

Downloadable Content

In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Fishbone cannot be put inside Bundled Supplies.

In the Hamlet DLC, its blueprint is dropped by the Queen Womant instead since Bees and Killer Bees are unavailable. The Ro Bin Gizzard Stone cannot be put inside Bundled Supplies.

Tips

  • Since unwrapping a Bundled Supplies returns the Wax Paper used to craft Bundling Wrap, the only net loss per use is 1 Rope.
  • Each held Bundling Wrap can be used to gain three extra inventory slots, useful when collecting a lot of resources and transporting said resources from one place to another.
  • A common use for Bundling Wraps is for storing Light Bulbs, as they no longer spoil quickly while bundled.

Trivia

  • Bundling Wraps and Bundled Supplies were first introduced to Don't Starve Together in the Herd Mentality update during A New Reign. They were later added to Don't Starve in the Quality of Life update.
  • According to Wickerbottom, Bundling Wrap has antibacterial effects. This may explain why bundled Food does not perish: Wax Paper neutralizes the microbes that cause spoilage.

Bugs

  • A bug may occur where a Bundled Supplies turns invisible, but remains present in a player's inventory.

Gallery

  • Bundling Wrap as seen in the poster for the Herd Mentality update.
  • Bundled Supplies of different sizes.
  • The interface that appears when using Bundling Wrap
DSTDSRoGSWHam

Exclusive to:
“Wrapping things up should make them easier to carry.”
–Wilson
“We could hide some gross things, I guess.”
–Willow
“Is for hiding goodies.”
–Wolfgang
“Abigail always helped me tie the bow.”
–Wendy
“FOR WRAPPING THINGS IN BUNDLES”
–WX-78
“Antibacterial wrapping for food preservation.”
–Wickerbottom
“The paper's brown. Like wood.”
–Woodie
“I guess we could wrap a few things up.”
–Maxwell
“The consolidation of rope and paper makes for good wrapping.”
–Wagstaff
“That's a wrap.”
–Wigfrid
“Let's cocoon something for later!”
–Webber
“That's a wrap!”
–Walani
“A good food wrap.”
–Warly
“Ol' Woodlegs can bury 'is tre'sure in't.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS FOR THE WRAPPING O' WILBA STUFFS”
–Wilba
“Put stuff inside”
–Wormwood
“Wrapped up extra tight so it'll fit in my pocket.”
–Wheeler
“We could wrap stuff up for later.”
–Winona
“Bind all cursed objects!”
–Wortox
“Pack lots of things, flurpt!”
–Wurt
“Efficient packing is truly an overlooked art in camping.”
–Walter
“It'll keep my things safe from the ravages of time.”
–Wanda

–Wurt

–Walter
“Bundled up for quick and efficient carrying.”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Gói Gói là một Vật phẩm Thẻ Sinh tồn chỉ có thể được tạo nguyên mẫu bằng cách sử dụng Bản thiết kế của nó. Nó yêu cầu 1 Giấy sáp và 1 Dây thừng để chế tạo, đồng thời dùng để truy cập vào 4 khe chứa đồ có thể được gói lại để tạo ra Vật tư đi kèm chứa bất kỳ vật phẩm nào được đặt bên trong.

The '''Bundling Wrap''' is a [[Survival Tab]] [[Item]] that can only be prototyped by using its [[Blueprint]]. It requires 1 [[Wax Paper]] and 1 [[Rope]] to craft, and is used to access 4 storage slots that can be wrapped up to create Bundled Supplies holding any items placed within.

Its Blueprint has a 4% chance to drop from a killed [[Bee]] or [[Killer Bee]]. Several items cannot be put inside Bundled Supplies, such as itself and [[Chester]]'s [[Eye Bone]]. Since [[Honeycomb]] is not renewable in ''Don't Starve'', only a limited number of Bundling Wrap can be crafted per world.

'''Bundled Supplies''' are created by wrapping up to 4 stacks of items with a Bundling Wrap. It can be stored in [[Chest]]s, [[:Category:Backpacks|Backpacks]] or the player's [[Inventory]]. Its appearance changes based on the number of storage slots used when wrapping items, regardless of the size of the stack in any slot or the type of item placed inside. [[Food]] items can be stored inside Bundled Supplies indefinitely without spoiling, providing a great deal of utility for survival. Unwrapping it will cause all the items stored inside to drop on ground, in addition to 1 Wax Paper.

=={{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content==
In the ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, the [[Fishbone]] cannot be put inside Bundled Supplies.

In the ''[[Hamlet]]'' DLC, its blueprint is dropped by the [[Queen Womant]] instead since Bees and Killer Bees are unavailable. The [[Ro Bin Gizzard Stone]] cannot be put inside Bundled Supplies.

=={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips==
* Since unwrapping a Bundled Supplies returns the Wax Paper used to craft Bundling Wrap, the only net loss per use is 1 Rope.
* Each held Bundling Wrap can be used to gain three extra inventory slots, useful when collecting a lot of resources and transporting said resources from one place to another.
* A common use for Bundling Wraps is for storing [[Light Bulb]]s, as they no longer spoil quickly while bundled.

=={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Bundling Wraps and Bundled Supplies were first introduced to ''Don't Starve Together'' in the ''[[A New Reign#November 24th, 2016 - Herd Mentality|Herd Mentality]]'' update during ''[[A New Reign]]''. They were later added to ''[[Don't Starve]]'' in the ''[[Don't Starve/Version History|Quality of Life]]'' update.
* According to [[Wickerbottom]], Bundling Wrap has antibacterial effects. This may explain why bundled Food does not perish: Wax Paper neutralizes the microbes that cause spoilage.

=={{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs ==
* A bug may occur where a Bundled Supplies turns invisible, but remains present in a player's inventory.
